agent_approach_summary: The agent traced the chat privilege path through `src/privileges/global.js`, `src/messaging/*`, `src/middleware/user.js`, account/profile helpers, and the admin i18n files, aiming to add a new `chat:privileged` permission and wire it into chat authorization. It explored the correct subsystems but never produced a patch.
reasoning: This looks clean, not contaminated. The problem statement explicitly says to introduce a global `chat:privileged` permission, notes that existing privilege sets do not expose it, and calls out UI visibility via i18n. The F2P tests check exactly those public-facing requirements: that the privilege appears in middleware-exposed privilege data, that global user/group privilege payloads include it, and that the source language file contains the corresponding i18n key. Those are all derivable from the spec. The tests do not lock the implementation to private helpers, exact internal control flow, or other undiscoverable details. While the gold patch also changes messaging enforcement, profile API `canChat`, and some unrelated files, the tests are not coupled to those extras; that is overpatch/undertesting noise, not contamination. The agent appears to have understood the intended feature and inspected the right code paths, but it never submitted code, so the failure is best explained by incomplete work / running out of time rather than unfair tests.
